1→/**
     2→ * EntityStore Types - STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 1
     3→ *
     4→ * Type definitions for the high-performance TypedArray-based entity store.
     5→ * Uses Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern for cache-friendly data access.
     6→ *
     7→ * Key principles:
     8→ * - TypedArrays for numeric data (cache-friendly, no GC pressure)
     9→ * - String IDs stored separately in Map<index, string>
    10→ * - Direct mutation (no Proxy, no reactive wrappers)
    11→ * - Zero allocations in hot path
    12→ */
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Task type enum values for TypedArray storage
    16→ * Stored as Uint8 (0-2)
    17→ */
    18→export const TASK_TYPE = {
    19→  MAINTENANCE: 0,
    20→  OVERHAUL: 1,
    21→  INSPECTION: 2,
    22→} as const;
    23→
    24→export type TaskTypeValue = (typeof TASK_TYPE)[keyof typeof TASK_TYPE];
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Task color enum values for TypedArray storage
    28→ * Stored as Uint8 (0-2)
    29→ */
    30→export const TASK_COLOR = {
    31→  BLUE: 0,
    32→  ORANGE: 1,
    33→  GRAY: 2,
    34→} as const;
    35→
    36→export type TaskColorValue = (typeof TASK_COLOR)[keyof typeof TASK_COLOR];
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Map from string TaskType to numeric value
    40→ */
    41→export const TASK_TYPE_TO_VALUE: Record<string, TaskTypeValue> = {
    42→  maintenance: TASK_TYPE.MAINTENANCE,
    43→  overhaul: TASK_TYPE.OVERHAUL,
    44→  inspection: TASK_TYPE.INSPECTION,
    45→};
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Map from numeric value to string TaskType
    49→ */
    50→export const VALUE_TO_TASK_TYPE: Record<TaskTypeValue, string> = {
    51→  [TASK_TYPE.MAINTENANCE]: 'maintenance',
    52→  [TASK_TYPE.OVERHAUL]: 'overhaul',
    53→  [TASK_TYPE.INSPECTION]: 'inspection',
    54→};
    55→
    56→/**
    57→ * Map from string TaskColor to numeric value
    58→ */
    59→export const TASK_COLOR_TO_VALUE: Record<string, TaskColorValue> = {
    60→  blue: TASK_COLOR.BLUE,
    61→  orange: TASK_COLOR.ORANGE,
    62→  gray: TASK_COLOR.GRAY,
    63→};
    64→
    65→/**
    66→ * Map from numeric value to string TaskColor
    67→ */
    68→export const VALUE_TO_TASK_COLOR: Record<TaskColorValue, string> = {
    69→  [TASK_COLOR.BLUE]: 'blue',
    70→  [TASK_COLOR.ORANGE]: 'orange',
    71→  [TASK_COLOR.GRAY]: 'gray',
    72→};
    73→
    74→/**
    75→ * TypedArray storage for task entities
    76→ * Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern for optimal cache utilization
    77→ *
    78→ * Memory layout for N tasks:
    79→ * - startDay: Float32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task
    80→ * - duration: Float32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task
    81→ * - trainIndex: Uint16Array(N) - 2 bytes per task
    82→ * - type: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task
    83→ * - color: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task
    84→ * - hasConflict: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task (0 or 1)
    85→ * - predecessorIndex: Int32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task (-1 = no predecessor)
    86→ *
    87→ * Total: 17 bytes per task = ~1.7MB for 100k tasks
    88→ * Compare to ~50MB+ for object-based storage with same data
    89→ */
    90→export interface TaskArrays {
    91→  /** Start day offset from dataset start date */
    92→  startDay: Float32Array;
    93→  /** Duration in days */
    94→  duration: Float32Array;
    95→  /** Train index (Y position) */
    96→  trainIndex: Uint16Array;
    97→  /** Task type (0=maintenance, 1=overhaul, 2=inspection) */
    98→  type: Uint8Array;
    99→  /** Task color (0=blue, 1=orange, 2=gray) */
   100→  color: Uint8Array;
   101→  /** Conflict flag (0=false, 1=true) */
   102→  hasConflict: Uint8Array;
   103→  /** Predecessor task index (-1 = no predecessor) */
   104→  predecessorIndex: Int32Array;
   105→}
   106→
   107→/**
   108→ * String data stored separately (cannot be in TypedArray)
   109→ * Uses index as key for O(1) lookup
   110→ */
   111→export interface TaskStringData {
   112→  /** Map from task index to task ID string */
   113→  ids: Map<number, string>;
   114→  /** Reverse lookup: task ID string to index */
   115→  idToIndex: Map<string, number>;
   116→}
   117→
   118→/**
   119→ * Entity store interface
   120→ * Provides typed access to SoA data
   121→ */
   122→export interface IEntityStore {
   123→  /** Current number of entities */
   124→  readonly count: number;
   125→  /** Maximum capacity */
   126→  readonly capacity: number;
   127→  /** TypedArray storage */
   128→  readonly arrays: TaskArrays;
   129→  /** String data storage */
   130→  readonly strings: TaskStringData;
   131→
   132→  /**
   133→   * Load entities from array of task objects
   134→   * Bulk load is optimized for initial data loading
   135→   */
   136→  load(tasks: TaskData[]): void;
   137→
   138→  /**
   139→   * Get task data by index (zero-allocation read)
   140→   * Returns a cursor that reads directly from arrays
   141→   */
   142→  get(index: number): TaskCursor;
   143→
   144→  /**
   145→   * Get task data by ID
   146→   * Returns null if not found
   147→   */
   148→  getById(id: string): TaskCursor | null;
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Clear all entities
   152→   */
   153→  clear(): void;
   154→
   155→  /**
   156→   * Get iterator over all valid entities
   157→   */
   158→  [Symbol.iterator](): Iterator<TaskCursor>;
   159→}
   160→
   161→/**
   162→ * Task data input format (from generator or API)
   163→ */
   164→export interface TaskData {
   165→  id: string;
   166→  trainIndex: number;
   167→  startDay: number;
   168→  duration: number;
   169→  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
   170→  color: 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';
   171→  hasConflict: boolean;
   172→  predecessorId?: string;
   173→}
   174→
   175→/**
   176→ * Task cursor - provides ergonomic read access to TypedArray data
   177→ * This is a lightweight view into the arrays, not a copy
   178→ *
   179→ * IMPORTANT: Do not store cursor references - they are invalidated
   180→ * when the underlying arrays are modified
   181→ */
   182→export interface TaskCursor {
   183→  /** Entity index in the arrays */
   184→  readonly index: number;
   185→  /** Task ID string */
   186→  readonly id: string;
   187→  /** Start day offset */
   188→  readonly startDay: number;
   189→  /** Duration in days */
   190→  readonly duration: number;
   191→  /** Train index */
   192→  readonly trainIndex: number;
   193→  /** Task type as numeric value */
   194→  readonly typeValue: TaskTypeValue;
   195→  /** Task type as string */
   196→  readonly type: string;
   197→  /** Task color as numeric value */
   198→  readonly colorValue: TaskColorValue;
   199→  /** Task color as string */
   200→  readonly color: string;
   201→  /** Whether task has conflict */
   202→  readonly hasConflict: boolean;
   203→  /** Predecessor task index (-1 if none) */
   204→  readonly predecessorIndex: number;
   205→  /** Predecessor task ID (undefined if none) */
   206→  readonly predecessorId: string | undefined;
   207→
   208→  // Computed properties for rendering
   209→  /** X position in world coordinates */
   210→  readonly x: number;
   211→  /** Y position in world coordinates */
   212→  readonly y: number;
   213→  /** Width in world coordinates */
   214→  readonly width: number;
   215→}

     2→ * Canvas State Machine
     3→ *
     4→ * Central state management for the MMS Canvas using @zazcart/tools.
     5→ * Uses useSyncExternalStore for React integration without stale closure issues.
     6→ *
     7→ * Slices:
     8→ * - viewport: Camera position, momentum, animation, panning
     9→ * - interaction: Hover and selection state for tasks
    10→ *
    11→ * @see TECH-DEBT-001
    12→ * @see F018 - Hover highlight
    13→ * @see F019 - Click to select

    19→

    29→  },
    30→  context: {

    51→  getState: canvasMachine.getState,

    54→};

1→/**
     2→ * Spatial Indexing Types for PERF-002
     3→ *
     4→ * This module defines interfaces for spatial queries on the canvas.
     5→ * The goal is to efficiently find entities within a viewport region
     6→ * without iterating through all 60k+ tasks.
     7→ *
     8→ * Architecture:
     9→ * - ISpatialIndex: Abstract interface for any spatial index implementation
    10→ * - SimpleSpatialIndex: O(n) placeholder (this PR)
    11→ * - RBushSpatialIndex: Full R-tree implementation (STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2)
    12→ *
    13→ * Coordinate system matches canvas:
    14→ * - X: days from start (positive = future)
    15→ * - Y: train rows (0 = top, negative = down)
    16→ */
    17→
    18→import type { TaskRender } from '../data/types';
    19→
    20→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→// VIEWPORT BOUNDS
    22→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Rectangle defining the visible viewport in world coordinates.
    26→ * Used for culling queries - only render entities within these bounds.
    27→ */
    28→export interface ViewportBounds {
    29→  /** Left edge in world X (days * pxPerDay) */
    30→  minX: number;
    31→  /** Right edge in world X */
    32→  maxX: number;
    33→  /** Bottom edge in world Y (negative, more trains) */
    34→  minY: number;
    35→  /** Top edge in world Y (closer to 0) */
    36→  maxY: number;
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Entity bounding box for spatial indexing.
    41→ * Compatible with RBush item format.
    42→ */
    43→export interface SpatialItem {
    44→  /** Left edge */
    45→  minX: number;
    46→  /** Right edge */
    47→  maxX: number;
    48→  /** Bottom edge (lower Y value, more negative) */
    49→  minY: number;
    50→  /** Top edge (higher Y value, closer to 0) */
    51→  maxY: number;
    52→  /** Reference to the entity ID */
    53→  id: string;
    54→}
    55→
    56→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    57→// SPATIAL INDEX INTERFACE
    58→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Query result with optional distance for nearest-neighbor queries.
    62→ */
    63→export interface SpatialQueryResult {
    64→  /** Entity ID */
    65→  id: string;
    66→  /** Distance from query point (for nearest queries) */
    67→  distance?: number;
    68→}
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Statistics for monitoring index performance.
    72→ */
    73→export interface SpatialIndexStats {
    74→  /** Total number of indexed items */
    75→  itemCount: number;
    76→  /** Time taken for last bulk load (ms) */
    77→  lastBuildTimeMs?: number;
    78→  /** Time taken for last query (ms) */
    79→  lastQueryTimeMs?: number;
    80→  /** Number of items returned in last query */
    81→  lastQueryResultCount?: number;
    82→}
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * Abstract interface for spatial indexing.
    86→ *
    87→ * This interface is implementation-agnostic - it can be backed by:
    88→ * - SimpleSpatialIndex: O(n) linear scan (placeholder)
    89→ * - RBushSpatialIndex: R-tree for O(log n) queries
    90→ * - FlatbushSpatialIndex: Static R-tree for read-heavy workloads
    91→ * - Custom TypedArray-based index for maximum performance
    92→ *
    93→ * Usage pattern:
    94→ * 1. Create index and load data once (or on data change)
    95→ * 2. Query frequently during pan/zoom
    96→ * 3. Clear and rebuild only when data changes
    97→ */
    98→export interface ISpatialIndex {
    99→  /**
   100→   * Bulk load items into the index.
   101→   * More efficient than inserting one by one.
   102→   *
   103→   * @param items - Array of items to index
   104→   */
   105→  load(items: SpatialItem[]): void;
   106→
   107→  /**
   108→   * Insert a single item into the index.
   109→   * Use sparingly - bulk load is more efficient.
   110→   *
   111→   * @param item - Item to insert
   112→   */
   113→  insert(item: SpatialItem): void;
   114→
   115→  /**
   116→   * Remove an item from the index by ID.
   117→   *
   118→   * @param id - ID of item to remove
   119→   * @returns true if item was found and removed
   120→   */
   121→  remove(id: string): boolean;
   122→
   123→  /**
   124→   * Clear all items from the index.
   125→   */
   126→  clear(): void;
   127→
   128→  /**
   129→   * Query items within viewport bounds.
   130→   * This is the main query used for viewport culling.
   131→   *
   132→   * @param bounds - Viewport rectangle in world coordinates
   133→   * @returns Array of item IDs within bounds
   134→   */
   135→  queryViewport(bounds: ViewportBounds): string[];
   136→
   137→  /**
   138→   * Query items within a circular region.
   139→   * Useful for mouse hover detection.
   140→   *
   141→   * @param x - Center X in world coordinates
   142→   * @param y - Center Y in world coordinates
   143→   * @param radius - Search radius in world units
   144→   * @returns Array of item IDs within radius
   145→   */
   146→  queryRadius(x: number, y: number, radius: number): string[];
   147→
   148→  /**
   149→   * Find the nearest item to a point.
   150→   * Useful for snapping and selection.
   151→   *
   152→   * @param x - Query X in world coordinates
   153→   * @param y - Query Y in world coordinates
   154→   * @param maxDistance - Maximum search distance (optional)
   155→   * @returns Nearest item ID and distance, or null if none found
   156→   */
   157→  queryNearest(x: number, y: number, maxDistance?: number): SpatialQueryResult | null;
   158→
   159→  /**
   160→   * Get performance statistics.
   161→   */
   162→  getStats(): SpatialIndexStats;
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   166→// HELPER FUNCTIONS
   167→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   168→
   169→/**
   170→ * Convert a TaskRender to a SpatialItem for indexing.
   171→ *
   172→ * @param task - Task with render coordinates
   173→ * @param pxPerDay - Pixels per day (default: 10)
   174→ * @param pxPerTrain - Pixels per train row (default: 24)
   175→ * @returns SpatialItem for indexing
   176→ */
   177→export function taskToSpatialItem(
   178→  task: TaskRender,
   179→  pxPerDay: number = 10,
   180→  pxPerTrain: number = 24
   181→): SpatialItem {
   182→  const minX = task.startDay * pxPerDay;
   183→  const maxX = (task.startDay + task.duration) * pxPerDay;
   184→  // Y is negative (trains go downward from 0)
   185→  const maxY = -task.trainIndex * pxPerTrain;
   186→  const minY = maxY - pxPerTrain;
   187→
   188→  return {
   189→    minX,
   190→    maxX,
   191→    minY,
   192→    maxY,
   193→    id: task.id,
   194→  };
   195→}
   196→
   197→/**
   198→ * Convert an array of TaskRender to SpatialItems.
   199→ *
   200→ * @param tasks - Array of tasks
   201→ * @param pxPerDay - Pixels per day
   202→ * @param pxPerTrain - Pixels per train row
   203→ * @returns Array of SpatialItems
   204→ */
   205→export function tasksToSpatialItems(
   206→  tasks: TaskRender[],
   207→  pxPerDay: number = 10,
   208→  pxPerTrain: number = 24
   209→): SpatialItem[] {
   210→  return tasks.map(task => taskToSpatialItem(task, pxPerDay, pxPerTrain));
   211→}
   212→
   213→/**
   214→ * Check if two rectangles intersect.
   215→ *
   216→ * @param a - First rectangle
   217→ * @param b - Second rectangle
   218→ * @returns true if rectangles overlap
   219→ */
   220→export function rectanglesIntersect(
   221→  a: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number },
   222→  b: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number }
   223→): boolean {
   224→  return a.minX <= b.maxX && a.maxX >= b.minX && a.minY <= b.maxY && a.maxY >= b.minY;
   225→}
   226→
   227→/**
   228→ * Calculate distance from a point to the center of a rectangle.
   229→ *
   230→ * @param px - Point X
   231→ * @param py - Point Y
   232→ * @param rect - Rectangle
   233→ * @returns Distance to center
   234→ */
   235→export function distanceToCenter(
   236→  px: number,
   237→  py: number,
   238→  rect: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number }
   239→): number {
   240→  const cx = (rect.minX + rect.maxX) / 2;
   241→  const cy = (rect.minY + rect.maxY) / 2;
   242→  return Math.sqrt((px - cx) ** 2 + (py - cy) ** 2);
   243→}
